raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A generic review of this research section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and baseline positioning are coherent above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The section advances an argument from prior latency baselines to ChronoCommit's claimed contribution, so checking premise-to-conclusion consistency is central.","R1.3":"The delegation implies checking whether the reported benchmark and abort-rate results support the claims made about latency and tradeoffs.","R1.4":"Because the section explicitly positions ChronoCommit against Spanner-style protocols and newer systems, novelty relative to prior work is a central review responsibility.","R1.6":"A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision as central expected responsibilities.","R1.7":"The artifact relies on named prior systems and citations, making reference accuracy, coverage, and attribution central to the review.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what a short review request entails without inventing unrequested duties.","RX.2":"The reviewer responsibility includes avoiding inflated judgments about ChronoCommit's contribution or the strength of cited baselines.","RX.3":"The task is only to project the delegation, so scope control is load-bearing.","RX.4":"Review responsibilities should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported comparisons that could harm downstream readers.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to specific language in the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A generic review of this research section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and baseline positioning are coherent above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The section advances an argument from prior latency baselines to ChronoCommit's claimed contribution, so checking premise-to-conclusion consistency is central.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation implies checking whether the reported benchmark and abort-rate results support the claims made about latency and tradeoffs.
rationale.R1.4: Because the section explicitly positions ChronoCommit against Spanner-style protocols and newer systems, novelty relative to prior work is a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision as central expected responsibilities.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on named prior systems and citations, making reference accuracy, coverage, and attribution central to the review.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what a short review request entails without inventing unrequested duties.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer responsibility includes avoiding inflated judgments about ChronoCommit's contribution or the strength of cited baselines.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to project the delegation, so scope control is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibilities should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported comparisons that could harm downstream readers.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific language in the delegation and artifact.
